facts: Classroom rules exist so that everyone is treated fairly; for example, taking turns with toys is a rule that makes sure no one is left out, which is fair.

A responsibility is something you are expected to do, like putting a book back on the shelf; when everyone does their responsibility, the classroom stays organized and everyone can find what they need.
Fairness does not always mean giving everyone the same thing; sometimes a student needs more help with a task, so getting extra help is fair because it helps them learn, just like everyone else.
misconceptions: If one student gets a different toy or more time than another, that is unfair because fairness means everyone gets exactly the same thing.
